text_chunk: Duration and Schedules of Internships  The external internships of students of the Faculty of Economics and Business are limited in terms of the number of hours, both overall and per training project, depending on the type of instruction. The breakdown of hours is set out in Annex 1.  Curricular internships: you may undertake as many curricular internships as the internship courses included in the curriculum of the corresponding program

text_chunk: . Each training project must include a minimum of 300 hours and a maximum of 375 hours.
text_chunk_norm: each training project must include a minimum of 300 hours and a maximum of 375 hours

text_chunk: Internships, both curricular and extracurricular, must be carried out within the academic period for which the enrollment is valid. Under no circumstances may they be extended or extend beyond the end of the official academic year of the Faculty and the University of Barcelona. This usually runs from September 15 of one year to September 14 of the following year.

text_chunk: Internships, both curricular and extracurricular, must be carried out Monday through Friday, excluding holidays, between 8:00 AM and 9:00 PM. The student may work a maximum of 6 hours per day

text_chunk: . The student may work a maximum of 6 hours per day. Exceptionally, they may work up to a maximum of 8 hours per day if they are in one of the following situations: if they have 12 credits left to complete their degree, excluding credits for the Final Degree Project, if they are a student in the EUS program or if the internship takes place during the months of July and August.

text_chunk: Economic Aspects: Each training project requires a financial contribution to the Faculty from the company or institution to cover the management, processing, and formalization of the internship training project, regardless of the duration of the student's internship period. This amount must be specified in each student's training project. Exemption from payment is always subject to the discretion of the Dean of the Faculty

text_chunk: . Exemption from payment is always subject to the discretion of the Dean of the Faculty. The internship agreement may provide that the company or institution remunerate the student, in the manner determined, as a scholarship or study aid. This amount must be indicated in the student's individual training plan for the practicum and must take into account the following: Extracurricular intership must always be remunerated

text_chunk: . Optional curricular internships must always be paid. Mandatory curricular internships, although it is recommended that they be paid, may not be. If the intership is remunerated, the minimum is 3 euros. Internships for statistics students are optional, and they can complete up to two.

text_chunk: MODALITY OF Bachelor’s Thesis. There are four modalities of Bachelor’s Thesis: Modality A: Bachelor’s Thesis carried out in the teaching centers.  Modality B: Bachelor’s Thesis carried out in companies or entities, associated with an educational cooperation agreement between the company and the university (External Internships program)

text_chunk: . Modality C: Bachelor’s Thesis carried out within the framework of mobility, through an academic exchange program.  Modality D: Bachelor’s Thesis carried out in companies or entities, carried out during an educational cooperation agreement, within a mobility framework associated with an academic exchange program.
text_chunk_norm: modality c bachelors thesis carried out within the framework of mobility through an academic exchange program modality d bachelors thesis carried out in companies or entities carried out during an educational cooperation agreement within a mobility framework associated with an academic exchange program

text_chunk: The Bachelor’s Thesis must be done individually. Exceptionally, the Head of Studies may authorize groups of two people, after written justification by the director of the Bachelor’s Thesis. The memory will have a maximum of 50 pages and may contain annexes in case you want to attach complementary information. This point does not affect the Bachelor’s Thesis of Double Degree ECO-EST.

As a guide, the student's dedication to the elaboration of works, must be 25h ' 1 credit, that is, a total of 18 ECTS25h / ECTS.450h of work.

text_chunk: The tribunal is responsible for evaluating the report of the work and the defense, in accordance with the objectives established in the teaching guide of the degree, and to communicate the qualification obtained to the student and the CTFG.

text_chunk: The members of the tribunal are proposed and appointed by the CTFG from among the teaching staff of the Departments that teach at the GE. The court is composed, at least, of: the president, who directs the entire process of action of the court; the secretary, who guards the evaluation act; a member; a substitute. Of the 4 members, two must be from the UB and two from the UPC

The indicative duration of the act will be 30 minutes, of which approximately 15 minutes will be devoted to the defense of the thesis.

text_chunk: Abstract and Keywords: A summary of the work in the language in which the Bachelor’s Thesis is written must be included, no more than 10 lines long, that synthesizes its essential content. Next, several keywords—no fewer than five and no more than ten—must be provided, indicating the topics addressed in the work

text_chunk: The submission of the thesis will be made in PDF format and as a single file, through the dropbox that will be set up in the Bachelor’s Thesis area of the Virtual Campus. Therefore, it will not be necessary to print the work unless the Bachelor’s Thesis advisor or the Bachelor’s Thesis Committee explicitly requests it. In that case, the work must be presented printed on both sides of white or recycled DIN-A4 sheets.

text_chunk: Requirements for UB students on international mobility   12.2 Calls for mobility require that UB students meet, at a minimum, the following requirements: b) provide proof of the language proficiency level required by the foreign institution, as specified in the agreement signed with the UB c) have a minimum of 60 credits completed for undergraduate studies (or the first year completed)

text_chunk: The mobility student is a full-time student; therefore, the number of credits that a UB student must take at the host university is, as a general rule, the one set by the UB's regulations on academic standing. For undergraduate students, between 23 and 30 ECTS for semester stays or between 46 and 60 ECTS for annual stays

If you have previously been a beneficiary of an Erasmus+ mobility (studies and/or internships), the total duration of the mobility per student may not exceed 12 months per cycle of studies (Bachelor's, Master's).

text_chunk: In the event of a tie between two or more candidates, the following tie-breaking criteria will be applied: First, applications will be ranked based on the number of ECTS credits passed, in descending order. If the tie persists, the spot will be awarded to the applicant who has not previously participated in a mobility program

text_chunk: General regulations of UB
text_chunk_norm: general regulations of ub

text_chunk: Information on the Grading Process: Grades for assessment activities must be made public no later than 14 calendar days after the tests have been administered or the assignments submitted.
text_chunk_norm: information on the grading process grades for assessment activities must be made public no later than 14 calendar days after the tests have been administered or the assignments submitted

text_chunk: The final grade for the course must be made public within a maximum of 14 calendar days from the date the continuous assessment processes, the single assessment exam, or the re-evaluation conclude. It must be ensured that the grades are published 5 calendar days before the re-evaluation date.
text_chunk_norm: the final grade for the course must be made public within a maximum of 14 calendar days from the date the continuous assessment processes the single assessment exam or the reevaluation conclude it must be ensured that the grades are published 5 calendar days before the reevaluation date

text_chunk: In the event that, for exceptional and duly justified reasons, a student or group of students cannot take an assessment, the course coordinator must take the necessary measures to ensure it can take place within the same academic period.
text_chunk_norm: in the event that for exceptional and duly justified reasons a student or group of students cannot take an assessment the course coordinator must take the necessary measures to ensure it can take place within the same academic period

text_chunk: A student with a grade of 9.0 or higher may be awarded an honors grade. The number of honors may not exceed 5% of the students enrolled in a course during the relevant academic period, unless the number of enrolled students is fewer than 20: in that case, only one honor may be awarded.
text_chunk_norm: a student with a grade of 90 or higher may be awarded an honors grade the number of honors may not exceed 5 of the students enrolled in a course during the relevant academic period unless the number of enrolled students is fewer than 20 in that case only one honor may be awarded

text_chunk: ECTS grading system at UB: On academic transcripts and on the European Supplement to the degree certificate for official bachelor's and master's degrees, ECTS grades are also indicated for passed courses, showing the ranking compared with the results of that same course in the two previous years
text_chunk_norm: ects grading system at ub on academic transcripts and on the european supplement to the degree certificate for official bachelors and masters degrees ects grades are also indicated for passed courses showing the ranking compared with the results of that same course in the two previous years

text_chunk: . Students who have passed a course are divided into five groups: the top 10% receive a grade of A; the next 25% receive a grade of B; the next 30% receive a grade of C; the next 25% receive a grade of D; and the final 10% receive a grade of E.
text_chunk_norm: students who have passed a course are divided into five groups the top 10 receive a grade of a the next 25 receive a grade of b the next 30 receive a grade of c the next 25 receive a grade of d and the final 10 receive a grade of e

text_chunk: Once a course has been graded, it cannot be re-evaluated unless the student waives the grade obtained, provided it is a passing grade or an equivalent or higher grade. This waiver must be submitted in writing to the relevant instructor during the grade review period and results in a grade of “not presented.”
text_chunk_norm: once a course has been graded it cannot be reevaluated unless the student waives the grade obtained provided it is a passing grade or an equivalent or higher grade this waiver must be submitted in writing to the relevant instructor during the grade review period and results in a grade of not presented

text_chunk: If the student commits any other irregularity in an assessment, that assessment is graded 0. If more than one irregularity occurs in the assessments of the same course during the same academic year, the final grade is 0.
text_chunk_norm: if the student commits any other irregularity in an assessment that assessment is graded 0 if more than one irregularity occurs in the assessments of the same course during the same academic year the final grade is 0

text_chunk: University Academic Progression Regulations
text_chunk_norm: university academic progression regulations

text_chunk: Enrollment and retention conditions during the first year of entry into a university degree program: In the first year a student enters a UB degree program, they must enroll in either 60 credits (full-time) or 30 (part-time mode). If they have enrolled in 60 credits, by the end of the first year they must have passed at least 18 credits to remain in the degree program

text_chunk: . If they have enrolled in 30 credits, by the end of the first year they must have passed at least 6 credits to remain in the degree program.
text_chunk_norm: if they have enrolled in 30 credits by the end of the first year they must have passed at least 6 credits to remain in the degree program

text_chunk: Enrollment and retention conditions from the second year of admission and for first-year students who have previously taken, in whole or in part, other official university studies at any university: The student may enroll in a minimum of 18 credits (unless fewer are needed to complete the program) and a maximum of 60
text_chunk_norm: enrollment and retention conditions from the second year of admission and for firstyear students who have previously taken in whole or in part other official university studies at any university the student may enroll in a minimum of 18 credits unless fewer are needed to complete the program and a maximum of 60

text_chunk: . Students are considered part-time when they enroll in between 18 and 45 credits, and full-time when they enroll in between 46 and 60 credits. Exceptionally, and when justified, the Head of Studies, by express resolution, may authorize enrollment of more than 60 credits, up to a maximum of 78 credits per academic year

text_chunk: . If the student wants to enroll in required credits from an upper-level course, they must also enroll in all outstanding required credits from lower-level courses. text_chunk: Enrollment and retention conditions for the first year of entry into the dual-degree undergraduate program: Full-time track: in the first year the student must enroll in a minimum of 60 credits and a maximum of 90. Part-time mode: in the first year the student must enroll in between a minimum of 30 credits and a maximum of 45

text_chunk: Limit on enrollment duration in a bachelor's program or in a dual bachelor's degree track.In 240-credit degree programs, a full-time student has a maximum of seven years of effective enrollment to complete the studies. text_chunk: For the purposes of the preceding sections, full-time enrollment is defined as registering an annual average of forty-six credits or more during the first five years of effective enrollment; if this average is below forty-six credits, enrollment is considered part-time.

text_chunk: Withdrawal from Studies. A student is considered to have withdrawn from a bachelor's degree, a university master's degree, or a dual-degree program at the bachelor's or master's level when two consecutive academic years have passed without enrollment. If a student has withdrawn from the program or the dual-degree track after having passed some credits, they may apply for readmission to the dean's office.

Variable amount

text_chunk: Students who belong to a large family (general category) are entitled to a 50% reduction in tuition fees for enrolled credits, as well as in the academic record management and certificate issuance fees. Students who belong to a large family (special category) are entitled to a 100% exemption from these fees.

text_chunk: The decree of the Government of Catalonia establishing the prices for the provision of academic services states that students who have been awarded an honors distinction or an extraordinary prize in upper secondary education or higher-level vocational training are entitled to full exemption from public fees for all credits in which they enroll for the first time during the first year of their official university studies.
